query: Can you point me towards research on contrastive learning methods used for fine-tuning sentence representations, where in-batch negatives may sometimes unintentionally be similar to the positive examples?
criteria: contrastive learning fine-tuning sentence representations in-batch negatives similar to positives
in-batch negative sampling issues contrastive learning sentence embeddings
false negatives in-batch contrastive learning sentence representation fine-tuning

title.value: Debiased Contrastive Learning of Unsupervised Sentence Representations

recommendation_reason.value: To address it, we present a new framework DCLR (Debiased Contrastive Learning of unsupervised sentence Representations) to alleviate the influence of these improper negatives.

supported_research_question.value: Recently, contrastive learning has been shown to be effective in improving pre-trained language models (PLM) to derive high-quality sentence representations.

method.value: we design an instance weighting method to punish false negatives and generate noise-based negatives to guarantee the uniformity of the representation space.

data_or_scenario.value: previous works mostly adopt in-batch negatives or sample from training data at random.

main_conclusion.value: Experiments on seven semantic textual similarity tasks show that our approach is more effective than competitive baselines.

limitations.value: However, previous works mostly adopt in-batch negatives or sample from training data at random.

relevance_strength.value: improper negatives (e.g., false negatives and anisotropy representations) are used to learn sentence representations

title.value: Contrastive Learning with Hard Negative Samples

recommendation_reason.value: We consider the question: how can you sample good negative examples for contrastive learning?

supported_research_question.value: We argue that, as with metric learning, learning contrastive representations benefits from hard negative samples (i.e., points that are difficult to distinguish from an anchor point).

method.value: we develop a new class of unsupervised methods for selecting hard negative samples where the user can control the amount of hardness.

data_or_scenario.value: For instance, on certain computer vision tasks unsupervised contrastive learning methods have even outperformed supervised pre-training [30,20].

main_conclusion.value: The proposed method improves downstream performance across multiple modalities, requires only few additional lines of code to implement, and introduces no computational overhead.

limitations.value: The key challenge toward using hard negatives is that contrastive methods must remain unsupervised, making it infeasible to adopt existing negative sampling strategies that use label information.

relevance_strength.value: The key challenge toward using hard negatives is that contrastive methods must remain unsupervised, making it infeasible to adopt existing negative sampling strategies that use label information.

title.value: PCL: Peer-Contrastive Learning with Diverse Augmentations for Unsupervised Sentence Embeddings

recommendation_reason.value: Learning sentence embeddings in an unsupervised manner is fundamental in natural language processing.

supported_research_question.value: Learning sentence embeddings in an unsupervised manner is fundamental in natural language processing.

method.value: Learning sentence embeddings in an unsupervised manner is fundamental in natural language processing.

data_or_scenario.value: Experiments on STS benchmarks verify the effectiveness of PCL against its competitors in unsupervised sentence embeddings.

main_conclusion.value: PCL performs peer-positive contrast as well as peer-network cooperation, which offers an inherent anti-bias ability and an effective way to learn from diverse augmentations.

limitations.value: A straightforward solution is resorting to more diverse positives from a multiaugmenting strategy, while an open question remains about how to unsupervisedly learn from the diverse positives but with uneven augmenting qualities in the text field.

relevance_strength.value: Learning sentence embeddings in an unsupervised manner is fundamental in natural language processing.
